title: Ferno iNX Series Stretcher - Hydraulic lift or height adjustment failure
description: Troubleshooting a stretcher that will not raise or lower due to hydraulic or mechanical issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from active patient use before troubleshooting. Expected: Patient safely transferred to another device.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Operate the lift controls (handle, pedal, or release mechanism) and observe movement. Expected: Confirmed failure, sluggish movement, or no response. Why it matters: Confirms true hydraulic issue vs user operation error.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions or Load Issues
steps.instructions: Inspect undercarriage and frame for debris, bent components, or interference. Ensure load is within rated capacity. Expected: No physical obstruction or excessive load. Why it matters: Mechanical blockage can prevent proper lift operation. If cleared and function returns → stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Proper Operation of Release/Control Mechanism
steps.instructions: Operate the lift handle or release lever while observing linkage movement. Expected: Smooth mechanical engagement of lift mechanism. Why it matters: A disconnected or misaligned linkage can prevent hydraulic actuation. If linkage is loose or not engaging → note for escalation.

steps.title: Inspect for Hydraulic Resistance or Free Movement
steps.instructions: Attempt to raise/lower while feeling for resistance or dead travel. Expected: Controlled resistance during operation. Why it matters: No resistance may indicate hydraulic fluid loss or internal failure, while excess resistance may indicate binding or mechanical restriction.

steps.title: Check for Hydraulic Fluid Leaks
steps.instructions: Inspect hydraulic cylinder, lines, and surrounding frame for oil residue or wetness. Expected: No visible leaks. Why it matters: Fluid loss leads to loss of lifting capability. If leak is present → remove from service.

steps.title: Evaluate Lift Speed and Stability
steps.instructions: Observe if movement is slow, uneven, or drifting under load. Expected: Smooth and stable height adjustment. Why it matters: Internal seal wear or air in the system can cause instability. If unstable → likely internal hydraulic issue.

steps.title: Cycle the Lift Multiple Times (No Load)
steps.instructions: Raise and lower the stretcher fully without a patient. Expected: Consistent performance through full range. Why it matters: Helps identify intermittent or partial hydraulic failure. If performance improves → possible minor air or temporary issue.

CCR.Complaint: Stretcher would not raise or lower during patient transport.
CCR.Cause: Hydraulic system showed no resistance and visible fluid leak at cylinder.
CCR.Resolution: Removed stretcher from service and sent to vendor for hydraulic repair.

helpfulDetails: Load conditions at time of failure
Final device status (in service vs removed)

title: Ferno iNX Series Stretcher - Cot not locking or latching properly
description: Troubleshooting cot locking and latching issues related to obstruction, alignment, linkage, and component wear.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from active patient use before troubleshooting. Expected: Patient safely transferred to another device.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Operate the cot through full raise/lower cycles and attempt to engage the latch at multiple positions. Expected: Clear confirmation of failure to lock or inconsistent latching. Why it matters: Confirms true mechanical issue vs improper operation.

steps.title: Inspect for Obstructions in Locking Mechanism
steps.instructions: Check latch points, hooks, and frame contact areas for debris, linen, or foreign objects. Expected: Clean, unobstructed locking surfaces. Why it matters: Even small debris can prevent full latch engagement. If cleared and latch works → stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check Alignment of Frame and Latch Points
steps.instructions: Visually inspect for bent components, misalignment, or uneven frame positioning. Gently cycle the cot while observing how latch components meet. Expected: Proper alignment with smooth engagement. Why it matters: Misalignment prevents the latch from fully seating. If alignment corrected and function returns → stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Latch Release Handle / Cable Operation
steps.instructions: Operate the release handle and observe cable or linkage movement. Expected: Smooth movement with full return to resting position. Why it matters: A partially engaged release mechanism can prevent locking. If cable is sticking, loose, or not returning → note for escalation.

steps.title: Check for Wear on Latch Components
steps.instructions: Inspect latch hooks, pins, and engagement surfaces for excessive wear, rounding, or damage. Expected: Sharp, intact contact surfaces. Why it matters: Worn components may not securely hold under load. If worn → remove from service.

steps.title: Test Under Light Load (No Patient)
steps.instructions: Apply light downward pressure while engaging the latch. Expected: Secure lock without slipping or disengaging. Why it matters: Confirms latch integrity without risking patient safety. If latch slips or fails → escalate.

CCR.Complaint: Stretcher cot not locking properly when raised; staff reports slipping during positioning.
CCR.Cause: Debris and partial obstruction found in latch engagement area preventing full lock.
CCR.Resolution: Cleared obstruction, verified proper latch engagement, and returned stretcher to service.

helpfulDetails: Condition of latch components (worn, bent, intact)

title: Ferno iNX Series Stretcher - Wheel or Loading System Malfunction
description: Troubleshooting guide for a stretcher that is difficult to roll or has loading system issues due to brakes, debris, alignment, or mechanical problems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from active patient use before troubleshooting. Patient safely transferred to another device.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to roll the stretcher and operate the loading system. Confirm difficulty rolling, dragging, or failure to load/unload properly.

steps.title: Check Brake Status
steps.instructions: Ensure all brakes are fully disengaged and cycle them on and off. Wheels should roll freely when brakes are off.

steps.title: Inspect Wheels for Obstructions or Debris
steps.instructions: Check casters for debris wrapped around axles and spin each wheel. Wheels should spin freely without resistance.

steps.title: Check Wheel Alignment and Tracking
steps.instructions: Push stretcher forward and observe tracking. Inspect for bent caster forks or uneven wheel positioning.

steps.title: Inspect Steering/Directional Lock Mechanism
steps.instructions: Verify steering mode is properly engaged or disengaged and observe wheel response.

steps.title: Inspect Loading System Components (If Equipped)
steps.instructions: Check loading wheels, guide rails, and engagement points. Operate loading mechanism without a patient if safe.

steps.title: Check for Structural Damage or Bent Components
steps.instructions: Inspect frame, undercarriage, and wheel mounts for bending, cracks, or deformation.

CCR.Complaint: Stretcher difficult to roll and not loading smoothly into ambulance mount.
CCR.Cause: Debris wrapped around rear caster axle restricting wheel rotation.
CCR.Resolution: Removed debris, verified smooth wheel rotation and proper loading function.

helpfulDetails: Presence of debris or damage noted
Final device status (returned to service or removed)

title: Ferno iNX Series Stretcher - Battery or powered drive system failure (powered models)
description: Troubleshooting for stretcher power, battery charging, and powered drive system issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from active patient transport before troubleshooting. Expected: Patient safely transferred to another device.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to power on the stretcher and engage the powered drive system. Expected: Confirm no power, no drive engagement, or intermittent operation. Why it matters: Confirms true failure vs user operation error.

steps.title: Check Battery Charge Level
steps.instructions: Observe battery indicator or display status (if equipped). Expected: Battery shows adequate charge. If low or depleted → charge fully and retest. Why it matters: Low voltage is the most common cause of drive failure. If resolved → stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Charging Method and Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure the charger or dock is properly connected and fully seated. Expected: Charging indicator activates when connected. Why it matters: Misalignment or incomplete connection prevents charging. If corrected and charging begins → retest after charge.

steps.title: Check Power Source (Outlet or Dock)
steps.instructions: Test the outlet with another device or verify dock power indicators. Expected: Confirmed working power source. If no power → move to known-good outlet or powered dock. Why it matters: Eliminates facility power issues.

steps.title: Inspect Charging Cable and Contacts
steps.instructions: Check for frayed cables, bent prongs or pins, and dirty or corroded contacts. Expected: Clean, intact connections with proper alignment. Clean contacts if dirty. If damaged → remove from service. Why it matters: Poor electrical contact prevents charging and operation.

steps.title: Verify Battery Seating and Connection
steps.instructions: Inspect battery compartment to ensure battery is fully seated and secured. Expected: Battery properly installed with no looseness. Why it matters: Loose batteries can cause intermittent or no power. If reseated and power returns → stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Perform a Power Reset (If Applicable)
steps.instructions: Turn the stretcher off, wait 10–15 seconds, then power back on. Expected: System reinitializes and may restore function. Why it matters: Clears minor electronic faults.

steps.title: Attempt Drive Engagement Under Proper Conditions
steps.instructions: Ensure brakes are disengaged, correct drive control is used, and no overload condition exists. Expected: Drive motor engages normally. Why it matters: Safety interlocks can prevent drive activation.

steps.title: Observe for Indicators, Sounds, or Fault Behavior
steps.instructions: Check for error lights or codes, clicking without movement, or no response at all. Expected: Normal startup and drive response. Why it matters: Helps distinguish electrical vs mechanical vs control issue.

CCR.Complaint: Stretcher powered drive not engaging and unit not powering on consistently.
CCR.Cause: Battery found depleted and not charging due to poor dock connection.
CCR.Resolution: Corrected docking alignment, verified charging, and confirmed normal drive operation.

title: Ferno iNX Series Stretcher - Frame Alignment or Structural Binding Issues
description: Stretcher difficult to raise, lower, or move smoothly due to frame misalignment, binding, or external mechanical interference.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the stretcher from active use before troubleshooting. Expected: Patient safely transferred to another device.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Cycle the stretcher through full height adjustments and attempt to roll it. Expected: Confirm stiffness, uneven movement, or binding. Why it matters: Confirms true mechanical restriction vs normal resistance.

steps.title: Inspect for Obstructions in Frame and Undercarriage
steps.instructions: Check all pivot points, scissor arms, and frame rails for debris, linens, or foreign objects. Expected: No visible obstructions. Why it matters: Even small debris can cause binding in moving components. If obstruction removed and movement returns → stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check for Uneven or Twisted Frame Position
steps.instructions: Visually inspect both sides of the stretcher during movement. Look for uneven lifting or one side lagging. Expected: Symmetrical movement on both sides. Why it matters: Frame twisting or uneven load can cause binding. If load repositioning corrects issue → stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Load Distribution and Weight Limits
steps.instructions: Ensure weight is centered and within rated capacity. Remove any excess load and retest. Expected: Smooth operation under proper load conditions. Why it matters: Off-center or excessive weight can stress the frame and create binding. If corrected → stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Inspect Wheels and Casters for Alignment Issues
steps.instructions: Check that all wheels are properly aligned, not bent, and rotate freely. Expected: Wheels track straight and roll smoothly. Why it matters: Misaligned wheels can create resistance that feels like frame binding. If corrected → stop troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check Linkages and Moving Joints for Resistance
steps.instructions: Operate lift and movement controls while observing linkage motion. Expected: Smooth, consistent mechanical movement without hesitation. Why it matters: Binding at a joint can indicate misalignment or mechanical wear. If abnormal resistance is observed → note for escalation.

steps.title: Look for Signs of Structural Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect frame for bent metal, cracked welds, or deformation. Expected: Straight frame with no visible damage. Why it matters: Structural damage will cause persistent alignment issues and unsafe operation. If damage found → remove from service immediately.

CCR.Complaint: Stretcher difficult to raise and lower, with uneven movement reported by staff.
CCR.Cause: Frame binding caused by debris in undercarriage pivot point.
CCR.Resolution: Removed debris, verified smooth operation, and returned stretcher to service.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (in service or removed)
